---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), an agent service boundary (DDx
orchestrates work while the upstream agent routes providers/models), a persona system (bindable AI
personalities), a library registry (plugins with prompts, templates,
personas), and git-aware synchronization. This skill makes any
skills-compatible coding agent (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini
CLI, etc.) understand and operate the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of open beads. The *ready queue* is the subset
  with all dependencies closed. `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — a bead with at least one unclosed dependency.
  `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one agent invocation atom. `ddx run` calls upstream agent
  `Execute` once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — an upstream agent routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream agent. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries; the agent owns
  the concrete harness/provider/model routing.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "run work", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. Execute-bead does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first agent dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

# REACH-BACKFILL decomposition

Bead: ddx-83440482 — checks: systematic backfill — apply production-reachability across cli/ and resolve all violations

## Why decomposed (Step 0 size check)

This bead's contract is a sweep: file children per cluster, drain them, achieve zero violations. The bead's own description prescribes the workflow ("File one child bead per violation cluster… Drain the children"). A single execute-bead attempt cannot land 283 wire/delete decisions across 21 packages and still pass the bead's AC. Per the executor instructions: "A clean decomposition is a successful attempt."

## How violations were enumerated

The installed `production-reachability` check is diff-scoped (it flags only newly-added unreached symbols in the current diff range), so a base==head ac run reports `pass — no diff range`. For backfill we need an exhaustive scan, which is exactly what the underlying engine — `golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode` (RTA) — produces. Run from `cli/`:

    go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 -json ./...

That output is preserved verbatim at `initial-violations.json` and flattened to `initial-violations.tsv` (one row per unreached symbol: `package\tfile\tline\tsymbol`).

Total unreached symbols: **283** across **21** packages.

## Cluster grouping

One child bead per Go package (sensible, matches blame boundaries, keeps each child small enough to drain in a single attempt). Per-package counts:

| Child bead | Package | Count |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ddx-0131ebf0 | cmd | 69 |

Each child carries the per-package symbol list, the WIRE-or-DELETE decision rule from the parent, and a per-package AC (zero remaining unreached symbols + tests green).

Dependency edges: parent ddx-83440482 depends on all 21 children, so `ddx bead ready` will not surface the parent until every cluster lands.

## Cross-bead linkage

- **WIRE bead ddx-09d2990c** (already closed prior to this run) was annotated with a supersession note pointing at ddx-83440482 / ddx-83b8994f. Its 4 originally-cited unwired functions are confirmed still dead in the current scan and are absorbed into the `internal/agent` child:
  - `internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go:93` — `Ladder.Next`
  - `internal/agent/retry_policy.go:113` — `EvaluateRetryPolicy`
  - `internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go:170` — `RunWithLockBackoff`
  - `internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go:278` — `DispatchPostAttempt`
- The Codex audit additions named in the parent description (jsonlFallbackForCollection, Store.breakStaleLock, NewStoreWithBackend, WithSpokeHTTPClient, Server.execStore, GraphQL NewResolver) are subsumed by the per-package children for `internal/bead`, `internal/federation`, `internal/server`, and `internal/server/graphql`.

## What still has to happen for the parent to close

1. Each of the 21 children drained (each lands a wire or delete change for the symbols it owns; tests green; per-package deadcode count = 0 for that package).
2. Final `ddx ac run --check production-reachability` against the post-merge tip emits `status=pass` with no violations.
3. Final summary written to `.ddx/executions/<final-run-id>/reach-backfill-summary.md` with the AC#7 fields (total / wired / deleted / follow-ups / LOC delta).

That final summary belongs to the last run that closes the parent — not this attempt.

    24		"structural_validation_failed": true,
    25	}
    26	
    27	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
    28	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
    29	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
    30	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
    31		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
    32	}
    33	
    34	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
    35	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
    36	// locking out a provider for too long.
    37	//
    38	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
    39	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
    40	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
    41	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
    42	
    43	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
    44	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
    45		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
    46		if probeResult != "" {
    47			body += " probe=" + probeResult
    48		}
    49		if detail != "" {
    50			body += "\n" + detail
    51		}
    52		return body
    53	}
    54	
    55	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
    56	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
    57	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
    58	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
    59	
    60	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
    61	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
    62	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
    63	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
    64	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
    65		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
    66		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
    67		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
    68		Status     string  `json:"status"`
    69		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
    70		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
    71	}
    72	
    73	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
    74	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
    75	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
    76	type EscalationSummary struct {
    77		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
    78		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
    79		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
    80		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
    81	}
    82	
    83	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
    84	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
    85	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
    86	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
    87	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
    88	// SuccessStatus.
    89	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
    90		out := EscalationSummary{
    91			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
    92			WinningTier:    winningTier,
    93		}
    94		if out.WinningTier == "" {
    95			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
    96		}
    97		for _, a := range attempts {
    98			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
    99			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
   100				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
   101			}
   102		}
   103		return out
   104	}
   105	
   106	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
   107	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
   108	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
   109	type BeadEventAppender interface {
   110		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
   111	}
   112	
   113	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
   114	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
   115	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
   116	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
   117	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
   118	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
   119		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
   120			return nil
   121		}
   122		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
   123		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
   124		if err != nil {
   125			return err
   126		}
   127		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
   128			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
   129			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
   130			Body:      string(body),
   131			Actor:     actor,
   132			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
   133			CreatedAt: createdAt,
   134		})
   135	}

    41		"insufficient_quota",
    42		// Subprocess harness binary missing or unstartable
    43		"command not found",
    44		"executable file not found",
    45		"no such file or directory",
    46	}
    47	
    48	// IsInfrastructureFailure reports whether the given failure status + detail
    49	// indicates a transient infrastructure problem the model could not have
    50	// fixed. Only execution_failed can be infrastructure; other escalatable
    51	// statuses are semantic outcomes that should proceed through the tier ladder.
    52	// Returns false for statuses whose detail does not match any known
    53	// infrastructure pattern.
    54	//
    55	// Used by the execute-loop to decide whether to (a) defer the bead with a
    56	// retry-after and try the same tier later, or (b) burn through to the next
    57	// tier as the standard escalation policy.
    58	func IsInfrastructureFailure(status, detail string) bool {
    59		if status != "execution_failed" {
    60			return false
    61		}
    62		if detail == "" {
    63			return false
    64		}
    65		lower := strings.ToLower(detail)
    66		for _, p := range InfrastructureFailurePatterns {
    67			if strings.Contains(lower, p) {
    68				return true
    69			}
    70		}
    71		return false
    72	}
    73	
    74	// DefaultMaxCostUSD is the per-execute-loop dollar cap on accumulated billed
    75	// spend. Exceeding the cap halts further bead claiming so an execute-loop
    76	// run cannot silently burn through arbitrary openrouter / pay-per-token
    77	// credits. Local LLMs and subscription-bundled providers do not count
    78	// toward the cap (see CountsTowardCostCap).
    79	//
    80	// $100 is large enough not to interrupt normal subscription-driven work
    81	// (where billed cost stays at $0) and small enough to surface a clear stop
    82	// signal before runaway openrouter usage.
    83	const DefaultMaxCostUSD = 100.0
    84	
    85	// CostClassBilled is the HarnessInfo.CostClass value the agent uses for
    86	// pay-per-token providers. Free local providers report "free" and
    87	// subscription-bundled providers report "subscription".
    88	const CostClassBilled = "expensive"
    89	
    90	// CountsTowardCostCap returns true when an attempt's reported CostUSD
    91	// should accumulate against the loop's MaxCostUSD. Callers pass the
    92	// HarnessInfo of the harness that ran the attempt; when info is nil the
    93	// safe default is to count (treat unknown as billed).
    94	func CountsTowardCostCap(isLocal, isSubscription bool, costClass string) bool {
    95		if isLocal {
    96			return false
    97		}
    98		if isSubscription {
    99			return false
   100		}
   101		// CostClass "free" or "subscription" never bill; only "expensive" does.
   102		if costClass != "" && costClass != CostClassBilled {
   103			return false
   104		}
   105		return true
   106	}
   107	
   108	// HarnessBilledLookup reports whether a harness's reported CostUSD should
   109	// be accumulated against a CostCapTracker. Implementations typically
   110	// consult a service.ListHarnesses snapshot. A nil lookup is treated as
   111	// "count by default" (the safe option for unknown harnesses).
   112	type HarnessBilledLookup func(harnessName string) bool
   113	
   114	// CostCapTracker accumulates billed cost for a single execute-loop run
   115	// and reports when accumulated spend has reached the configured cap.
   116	// Safe for concurrent use across goroutines (workers may call Add and
   117	// Tripped concurrently).
   118	//
   119	// MaxUSD == 0 disables the cap; Tripped always returns (_, false). The
   120	// Lookup callback decides which harnesses contribute to the running
   121	// total — local and subscription-bundled providers should be excluded
   122	// (see CountsTowardCostCap).
   123	type CostCapTracker struct {
   124		MaxUSD float64
   125		Lookup HarnessBilledLookup
   126	
   127		mu    sync.Mutex
   128		spent float64
   129		// cache memoizes Lookup results so we do not rebuild a service for
   130		// every reported attempt. Protected by mu.
   131		cache map[string]bool
   132	}
   133	
   134	// NewCostCapTracker constructs a tracker with the given dollar cap and
   135	// lookup. Pass maxUSD <= 0 to disable the cap. A nil lookup is treated
   136	// as "count by default" — every harness contributes.
   137	func NewCostCapTracker(maxUSD float64, lookup HarnessBilledLookup) *CostCapTracker {
   138		return &CostCapTracker{
   139			MaxUSD: maxUSD,
   140			Lookup: lookup,
   141			cache:  map[string]bool{},
   142		}
   143	}
   144	
   145	// counts reports whether harnessName's CostUSD should accumulate. The
   146	// result is memoized; callers that want fresh resolution should
   147	// construct a new tracker. Lookups for empty harness names default to
   148	// true (count) so accidentally-missing harness metadata never silently
   149	// bypasses the cap.
   150	func (t *CostCapTracker) counts(harnessName string) bool {
   151		if harnessName == "" {
   152			return true
   153		}
   154		t.mu.Lock()
   155		if v, ok := t.cache[harnessName]; ok {
   156			t.mu.Unlock()
   157			return v
   158		}
   159		t.mu.Unlock()
   160		result := true
   161		if t.Lookup != nil {
   162			result = t.Lookup(harnessName)
   163		}
   164		t.mu.Lock()
   165		t.cache[harnessName] = result
   166		t.mu.Unlock()
   167		return result
   168	}
   169	
   170	// Add accumulates costUSD against the running total when harnessName's
   171	// billing class counts toward the cap. Non-positive cost is ignored.
   172	func (t *CostCapTracker) Add(harnessName string, costUSD float64) {
   173		if costUSD <= 0 {
   174			return
   175		}
   176		if !t.counts(harnessName) {
   177			return
   178		}
   179		t.mu.Lock()
   180		t.spent += costUSD
   181		t.mu.Unlock()
   182	}
   183	
   184	// Spent returns the current accumulated billed total.
   185	func (t *CostCapTracker) Spent() float64 {
   186		t.mu.Lock()
   187		defer t.mu.Unlock()
   188		return t.spent
   189	}
   190	
   191	// Tripped reports whether the accumulated spend has reached MaxUSD. When
   192	// MaxUSD <= 0 the cap is disabled and Tripped returns (_, false).
   193	// Returns the formatted operator-facing detail string when tripped so
   194	// callers can populate a stop-the-loop ExecuteBeadReport.
   195	func (t *CostCapTracker) Tripped() (string, bool) {
   196		if t.MaxUSD <= 0 {
   197			return "", false
   198		}
   199		t.mu.Lock()
   200		spent := t.spent
   201		t.mu.Unlock()
   202		if spent < t.MaxUSD {
   203			return "", false
   204		}
   205		return fmt.Sprintf("cost cap reached: $%.2f billed >= $%.2f cap; raise the cap or set 0 to disable. Subscription and local providers do not count.", spent, t.MaxUSD), true
   206	}

# DDx CLI Makefile

# Build variables
VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
COMMIT ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
LDFLAGS = -ldflags "-X main.version=${VERSION} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}"

# Build directory
BUILD_DIR = build

# Platforms to build for
PLATFORMS = \
	darwin/amd64 \
	darwin/arm64 \
	linux/amd64 \
	linux/arm64 \
	windows/amd64

.PHONY: all build clean test lint deps help obsidian-migrate obsidian-validate obsidian-test obsidian-dry-run obsidian-nav obsidian-revert bead-schema skill-schema gendocs frontend-build frontend-rebuild frontend-verify copy-skills eval-skill bench-graphql

# Default target
all: clean deps test build

# Build SvelteKit frontend (required before Go build for //go:embed).
# Skip rebuild when no source file is newer than the last build output.
FRONTEND_SRC := $(shell find internal/server/frontend/src internal/server/frontend/static -type f 2>/dev/null)
FRONTEND_STAMP := internal/server/frontend/build/index.html

frontend-build: $(FRONTEND_STAMP)

$(FRONTEND_STAMP): $(FRONTEND_SRC) internal/server/frontend/package.json internal/server/frontend/svelte.config.js internal/server/frontend/vite.config.ts
	@echo "Building SvelteKit frontend..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Force a frontend rebuild regardless of timestamps
frontend-rebuild:
	@echo "Rebuilding SvelteKit frontend (forced)..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Verify the embedded frontend bundle is internally consistent: every
# /_app/* asset referenced by index.html must exist on disk. Catches the
# class of bug where index.html and the chunk dir get out of sync (partial
# build, file restore, manual edit) and go:embed silently bakes in a
# broken bundle that 404s every JS chunk to the SPA shell.
frontend-verify:
	@echo "Verifying embedded frontend bundle integrity..."
	@cd internal/server/frontend/build && \
	missing=0; \
	for ref in $$(grep -hoE '"/_app/[^"]+"' index.html | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do \
		path=".$$ref"; \
		if [ ! -f "$$path" ]; then \
			echo "  MISSING: $$ref"; \
			missing=$$((missing+1)); \
		fi; \
	done; \
	if [ $$missing -gt 0 ]; then \
		echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing — run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  ok"

# Copy the canonical top-level skills/ddx/ tree into cli/internal/skills/ddx/
# so //go:embed (which cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks it up.
# Runs as a prereq of build/test/dev so contributors never see stale embedded
# skills. rsync --delete keeps the destination in exact sync.
SKILLS_SRC := ../skills/ddx
SKILLS_DST := internal/skills/ddx
copy-skills:
	@if [ ! -d "$(SKILLS_SRC)" ]; then \
		echo "error: $(SKILLS_SRC) not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ $(SKILLS_DST)/

# Build for current platform
build: copy-skills frontend-build
	@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .

# Build for all platforms
build-all: clean deps copy-skills
	@echo "Building for all platforms..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	@echo "Building current platform..."
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
	@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
		OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
		ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
		echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
		GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \
		if [ $$OS = "windows" ]; then \
			mv $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH.exe; \
		fi; \
	done

# Install dependencies
deps:
	@echo "Installing dependencies..."
	go mod download
	go mod tidy

# Run tests
test: copy-skills
	@echo "Running tests..."
	go test -v ./...

# Run the GraphQL perf harness (ddx-9ce6842a). Seeds a 10 × 500 bead fixture
# and records baseline numbers under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/. Override
# the fixture size or the report date via BENCH_REPORT_DATE.
BENCH_REPORT_DIR ?= $(abspath ../docs/helix/06-iterate/perf)
BENCH_REPORT_DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
bench-graphql:
	@echo "Running GraphQL perf harness (fixture: 10 × 500 beads = 5,000)..."
	@mkdir -p $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR=$(BENCH_REPORT_DIR) \
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DATE=$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE) \
	go test ./internal/server/perf/ -run TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline -v -timeout 5m
	@echo "Baseline report written to $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)/$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE)-baseline.md"

# Validate the shared bead record schema
bead-schema:
	@echo "Validating bead record schema..."
	go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestBeadRecordSchema' -count=1

# Validate bundled skill metadata
skill-schema:
	@echo "Validating bundled skill metadata..."
	go run . skills check ../skills internal/skills

# Run linter
lint:
	@echo "Running linter..."
	@if command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		golangci-lint run; \
	else \
		echo "golangci-lint not found, running go vet instead"; \
		go vet ./...; \
	fi
	@echo "Running routinglint (FEAT-006 anti-reintroduction gate)..."
	@go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...

# Install locally for development
install: build
	@echo "Installing DDx locally..."
	cp $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx $(HOME)/.local/bin/ddx
	@echo "DDx installed to ~/.local/bin/ddx"

# Clean build artifacts
clean:
	@echo "Cleaning..."
	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)

# Run the CLI (for development)
run:
	go run . $(ARGS)

# Format code
fmt:
	go fmt ./...

# Update dependencies
update-deps:
	go get -u all
	go mod tidy

# Create release archives
release: build-all
	@echo "Creating release archives..."
	@cd $(BUILD_DIR) && \
	for file in ddx-*; do \
		if [[ $$file == *.exe ]]; then \
			zip $${file%.*}.zip $$file; \
		else \
			tar -czf $$file.tar.gz $$file; \
		fi; \
	done

# Development server (watches for changes)
dev: copy-skills
	@echo "Starting development mode..."
	@if command -v air >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		air; \
	else \
		echo "Air not found. Install with: go install github.com/cosmtrek/air@latest"; \
		echo "Falling back to basic run..."; \
		go run . $(ARGS); \
	fi

# Obsidian integration targets
obsidian-migrate: build
	@echo "Migrating HELIX workflow to Obsidian format..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian migrate workflows/helix

obsidian-validate: build
	@echo "Validating Obsidian format in HELIX workflow..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian validate workflows/helix

obsidian-test:
	@echo "Running Obsidian integration tests..."
	go test ./internal/obsidian/... -v
	go test ./internal/obsidian/converter/... -v
	go test ./internal/obsidian/validator/... -v

obsidian-dry-run: build
	@echo "Running Obsidian migration dry-run..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian migrate --dry-run workflows/helix

obsidian-nav: build
	@echo "Generating navigation hub..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian nav workflows/helix

obsidian-revert: build
	@echo "Reverting Obsidian migration..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian revert --strip-frontmatter workflows/helix

# Generate Hugo markdown docs for every CLI command
gendocs:
